name: Heirloom Raisin Muffins
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine raisins and water in a saucepan. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and cover. Simmer for 20 minutes. Drain the raisins, reserving the liquid.
2. Add enough of the reserved liquid to make 1/2 cup. Allow to cool thoroughly.
3. In a bowl, cream together butter and sugar until light and fluffy using an electric mixer at medium speed. Add eggs, beating in for 2 more minutes.
4. Sift flour and baking powder together.
5. Alternately add the flour mixture with 1/2 cup of reserved raisin liquid into the butter mixture, mixing well after each addition.
6. Stir in the drained raisins. Spoon batter evenly into greased muffin pan cups, filling them about 2/3 full.
7. Bake at 400°F for 18 minutes or until golden brown. Serve hot with homemade jam or jelly.
